---
title: ""
description: "Social Epidemiology Lab — independent research and analytics in epidemiology, health economics, and policy."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<section class="lab-hero">
  <img class="lab-logo lab-logo-light" src="assets/lab-logo-color.png" alt="Social Epidemiology Lab logo">
  <img class="lab-logo lab-logo-dark"  src="assets/lab-logo-white.png" alt="Social Epidemiology Lab logo">
  <p class="lab-tagline">
    Turning health data into evidence that stands up in a manuscript, a boardroom, and a ministry.
  </p>
  <p class="lab-intro">
    The <strong>Social Epidemiology Lab</strong> is an independent research and analytics practice.
    We help universities, clinicians, NGOs, and public institutions design studies, analyse data
    rigorously, and translate the results into decisions — from a single 2×2 table to a national
    impact assessment. Every engagement is led personally by
    <a href="index.qmd">Dr. Kostadin Kostadinov</a>, MD, PhD, MPH.
  </p>
  <div class="lab-hero-cta">
    <a class="btn btn-secondary btn-lg" href="mailto:drkostadinkostadinov@gmail.com?subject=Social%20Epidemiology%20Lab%20enquiry" role="button">Request a consultation</a>
  </div>
</section>
```

## What we do

Six services, one standard: methodologically defensible, transparently documented, and reproducible.

::: {.grid .lab-services}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Epidemiological analysis
Measures of association, standardization, survival, and surveillance analytics — cohort, case–control, and cross-sectional designs handled end to end, with the right effect measure and the right confidence interval every time.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Technical & statistical analysis
Study-grade biostatistics: regression modelling, diagnostic accuracy, agreement and reliability, and publication-ready tables and figures — powered by our own open-source R toolkit, [kkstatfun](kkstatfun.qmd).
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Study design & data collection
Protocols, sample-size and power calculations, sampling strategies, questionnaire design, and reproducible data-collection pipelines — so the analysis is sound before the first record is gathered.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Health economics & HTA
Cost-of-illness, cost-effectiveness and cost-utility modelling, budget-impact and health-technology-assessment support — Markov models, ICERs, and probabilistic sensitivity analysis built to CHEERS standards.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Policy analysis & documents
Policy briefs, position papers, and evidence syntheses that translate findings into actionable recommendations — grounded in formal training in Impact Assessment & Policy Appraisal and ongoing study of law.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.lab-service}
### Impact assessment
Regulatory, health, and social impact assessments — ex-ante and ex-post — using structured intervention logic, multi-criteria analysis, and distributional analysis aligned with Better Regulation practice.
:::
:::

:::

## Why work with us

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Academic rigour
Every method is one we teach and publish. Analyses are defensible under peer review, not black boxes.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Fully reproducible
Code, data dictionaries, and versioned outputs come with the deliverable — your results can be re-run and audited.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Decision-focused
We don't stop at a p-value. Findings arrive framed for the manuscript, the committee, or the regulator who needs them.
:::

:::

## Selected expertise

The Lab draws on active academic work in infectious-disease surveillance, air-quality and environmental epidemiology, socio-economic burden-of-disease studies, and health-technology assessment — with results presented at ISPOR, EPH, and ECDC forums and published in peer-reviewed journals. See the full record on the [CV](CV.qmd).

```{=html}
<section class="lab-cta">
  <h2>Have a project in mind?</h2>
  <p>Tell us the question — we'll tell you the cleanest way to answer it.</p>
  <a class="btn btn-secondary btn-lg" href="mailto:drkostadinkostadinov@gmail.com?subject=Social%20Epidemiology%20Lab%20enquiry" role="button">Get in touch</a>
</section>
```
